criteria.t: A1. Confirmed RA diagnosis by rheumatologist
criteria.why: Rheumatologist documented moderate-to-severe RA diagnosis with serology and exam findings.

src.q: Assessment: moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis

criteria.t: A2. 3+ month conventional DMARD trial, inadequate response/intolerance
criteria.why: Methotrexate used 5+ months with documented inadequate response despite tolerance.

src.q: Tolerating methotrexate without adverse effects but response inadequate
methotrexate 20 mg weekly since September 2025

criteria.t: A3. Negative TB screening within 12 months
criteria.why: No TB test result present anywhere in record; screening status undocumented.

src.q: No tuberculosis screening result on file

rationale: A1 and A2 fully met with direct rheumatologist documentation and claims-verified DMARD trial. A3 missing — no TB screening result documented anywhere in record, so PEND pending TB test.

confidence.evidence: medium (TB screening data absent, not just negative)

criteria.why: 8 wks PT + NSAIDs completed, pain unchanged.

src.q: 8 wks PT completed, pain unchanged 7/10
NSAIDs used throughout course with minimal relief

criteria.why: Positive SLR + dermatomal sensory loss on exam.

src.q: SLR positive at 40 degrees, decreased sensation L5 dermatome left foot

criteria.why: ROS negative for fever/trauma/malignancy, no cauda equina signs.

src.q: Review of systems negative for fever, weight loss, trauma, or history of malignancy
No bowel or bladder dysfunction

rationale: All three criteria met: documented conservative therapy failure, objective radicular findings, no red flags. MRI ordering physician also confirmed conservative management failed.
policy: IMG-0221 v2.1 (eff 2025-11-01) — Lumbar spine MRI medical necessity

criteria.t: B1: Confirmed PsA diagnosis (CASPAR) by rheumatologist
criteria.why: Rheumatologist documents CASPAR-criteria PsA diagnosis with exam findings.

src.q: Meets CASPAR criteria, dactylitis present, nail pitting

criteria.t: B2: 3-month conventional DMARD trial, inadequate response, verifiable in medication/claims history
criteria.why: Clinician note reports a sulfasalazine trial with inadequate response, but pharmacy claims show no sulfasalazine fill in the same period.

src.q: tolerated sulfasalazine x4 months from an outside provider with inadequate response
No sulfasalazine claims found 2024-2026

criteria.t: B3: Negative TB screening within 12 months preceding request
criteria.why: QuantiFERON-TB Gold negative result on file within the required window.

src.q: QuantiFERON-TB Gold 2026-04-11: negative

rationale: B1 and B3 are satisfied, but B2 is contradicted by conflicting sources (self-reported DMARD trial vs. absent claims history), so this requires clinician review rather than auto-approval or a documentation-pend.

confidence.evidence: medium (conflict between self-report and claims data on DMARD trial)

criteria.why: Radiograph found solid nodule, confirmed in pulm note.

src.q: 7 mm solid nodule right upper lobe, incidentally noted
7 mm nodule seen on chest radiograph December 2025

criteria.why: High-risk pt, 6-8mm nodule, guidance 6-12mo, requested at 7mo fits.

src.q: Per Fleischner guidance for a 6-8 mm solid nodule in a high-risk patient, CT follow-up at 6-12 months is indicated; now at 7 months

criteria.t: No duplicate chest CT within 3 months
criteria.why: Imaging history confirms no chest CT on record at all.

src.q: No chest CT on record 2023-2026

rationale: All three criteria met with concordant prior-imaging, Fleischner-interval, and duplicate-check evidence, so approve. No missing docs.
policy: IMG-0450 v1.3 (eff 2025-08-01) — Chest CT with contrast for pulmonary nodule follow-up

criteria.t: C1 Confirmed moderate-to-severe Crohn's dx by GI
criteria.why: Gastroenterologist documents SES-CD 12, exceeding threshold of 7.

src.q: moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease, SES-CD 12 on colonoscopy 2026-06-10

criteria.why: Azathioprine trial ~5 months with persistent symptoms confirms inadequate response.

src.q: On azathioprine since January 2026 with partial improvement; symptoms persist

criteria.why: QuantiFERON negative result dated within 12 months of request.

src.q: QuantiFERON-TB Gold 2026-05-02: negative

rationale: All three Section C criteria met with direct verbatim documentation — confirmed dx w/ SES-CD, adequate DMARD trial duration w/ persistent symptoms, negative TB screen. No gaps, no conflicts.
